triageFlags.title: Excessive Debt-to-Income Ratio
triageFlags.description: The applicant's DTI ratio is significantly above the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Decline the application due to the unsustainable DTI ratio.
triageFlags.reasoning: According to the policy, the maximum DTI for Tier C applicants is 35%. The applicant's DTI ratio of 440% far exceeds this limit, indicating severe affordability issues.

triageFlags.title: Sub-Prime Credit Score
triageFlags.description: The applicant's credit score places them in the 'Sub-Prime' category, requiring stricter lending criteria.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider declining the application or reassessing under stricter conditions.
triageFlags.reasoning: The credit score of 575 categorizes the applicant as 'Sub-Prime' (Tier C), which requires stricter lending criteria, including a lower DTI ratio and potentially higher deposit requirements.

content.reasoning: The applicant's DTI ratio significantly exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier, indicating a high risk of financial strain. Additionally, the credit score is within the 'Sub-Prime' range, necessitating stricter lending criteria, which are not met in this proposal.
